tagline: Calculate the right speaker wire gauge for your setup
keywords: speaker wire, wire gauge, AWG, speaker cable, impedance
calcFn: var ratio = distance / impedance; var gauge = ratio > 50 ? 10 : ratio > 25 ? 12 : ratio > 12 ? 14 : 16; var resistance = {'10': 0.001, '12': 0.0016, '14': 0.0025, '16': 0.004}; var wireR = resistance[String(gauge)] * distance * 2; var maxLoss = (wireR / (wireR + impedance)) * 100; return {gauge: fmt(gauge,0), maxLoss: fmt(maxLoss,1)};

faq.q: Does wire gauge matter for speakers?
faq.a: Yes, thicker wire (lower gauge) reduces signal loss over long runs.
For runs over 50 feet with 8-ohm speakers, or 25+ feet with 4-ohm speakers.

formulas.name: Signal Loss
formulas.formula: Loss% = Wire Resistance / (Wire R + Speaker R) × 100
formulas.explanation: Proportion of power lost in the wire versus delivered to speaker.

tagline: Calculate acoustic panel coverage for your room
keywords: acoustic treatment, sound panels, room acoustics, studio
calcFn: var wallArea = 2*(length+width)*height; var coverPct = {'Music Listening': 0.25, 'Home Studio': 0.40, 'Podcast Room': 0.50, 'Home Theater': 0.30}; var pct = coverPct[purpose] || 0.3; var panelCoverage = wallArea * pct; var panels = Math.ceil(panelCoverage / 8); return {wallArea: fmt(wallArea,0), panelCoverage: fmt(panelCoverage,0), panels: fmt(panels,0)};

faq.q: How much acoustic treatment do I need?
faq.a: 25-40% wall coverage for listening rooms, 40-60% for recording studios.
First reflection points, behind speakers, and in corners (bass traps).

formulas.name: Coverage
formulas.formula: Panels = Wall Area × Coverage% / Panel Size
formulas.explanation: Total wall area times treatment percentage divided by panel area.

tagline: Calculate enclosure volume for your subwoofer
keywords: subwoofer box, enclosure volume, bass, speaker box
calcFn: var volumes = {'8': {'Sealed':0.35,'Ported':0.5,'Bandpass':0.7}, '10': {'Sealed':0.6,'Ported':1,'Bandpass':1.4}, '12': {'Sealed':1,'Ported':1.75,'Bandpass':2.5}, '15': {'Sealed':2,'Ported':3.5,'Bandpass':5}, '18': {'Sealed':3.5,'Ported':6,'Bandpass':8}}; var vol = volumes[driverSize][enclosureType] * drivers; var side = Math.pow(vol * 1728, 1/3); var dimensions = fmt(side,0) + ' x ' + fmt(side,0) + ' x ' + fmt(side,0) + ' inches (cube)'; var surfaceArea = 6 * side * side / 144; var woodSheets = Math.ceil(surfaceArea / 32); return {volume: fmt(vol,2), dimensions: dimensions, woodSheets: fmt(woodSheets,0)};

faq.a: Sealed: tighter bass, smaller box. Ported: louder, deeper bass, bigger box.
Absolutely. Wrong volume changes frequency response, making bass boomy or thin.

formulas.name: Box Volume
formulas.formula: Volume based on driver size × enclosure type × number of drivers
formulas.explanation: Manufacturer specs determine optimal volume per driver.

tagline: Estimate audio file size for podcast episodes
keywords: podcast, file size, audio, bitrate, MP3
calcFn: var bitrates = {'64 (mono speech)': 64, '96 (mono music)': 96, '128 (stereo standard)': 128, '192 (stereo high)': 192, '320 (stereo max)': 320}; var br = bitrates[bitrate] || 128; if (format === 'WAV (uncompressed)') br = 1411; if (format === 'AAC') br = br * 0.8; var fileSize = (br * duration * 60) / 8 / 1024; var bandwidth = fileSize * 1000 / 1024; return {fileSize: fmt(fileSize,1), bandwidth: fmt(bandwidth,1)};

faq.a: 64 kbps mono for speech, 128 kbps stereo for music-heavy shows.
AAC sounds better at the same bitrate but MP3 has wider compatibility.

formulas.name: File Size
formulas.explanation: Bitrate times duration in seconds, converted to megabytes.

tagline: Calculate delay timing for speaker alignment
keywords: speaker delay, audio alignment, time alignment, PA system
calcFn: var tempC = (temperature - 32) * 5/9; var soundSpeed = 1086 + (tempC * 1.14); var delayMs = (distance / soundSpeed) * 1000; return {delayMs: fmt(delayMs,1), soundSpeed: fmt(soundSpeed,0)};

faq.a: Speakers at different distances need timing offsets so sound arrives at listeners simultaneously.
Yes, sound travels faster in warmer air, changing the required delay.

formulas.name: Delay
formulas.explanation: Time for sound to travel the offset distance.

tagline: Check if your turntable speed is accurate
calcFn: var target = parseFloat(targetRpm); var error = ((measuredRpm - target) / target) * 100; var pitchShift = error * 17.31; var status = Math.abs(error) < 0.5 ? 'Accurate' : Math.abs(error) < 1 ? 'Slightly off' : 'Needs adjustment'; return {error: (error >= 0 ? '+' : '') + fmt(error,2), pitchShift: (pitchShift >= 0 ? '+' : '') + fmt(pitchShift,1), status: status};

faq.q: How accurate should turntable speed be?
faq.a: Within 0.5% is good, within 0.1% is excellent.
Use a strobe disc or smartphone app while the platter spins.

formulas.name: Speed Error
formulas.explanation: Percentage deviation from the correct speed.

tagline: Calculate a pitcher's earned run average
keywords: ERA, baseball, pitching stats, earned run average
calcFn: var era = (earnedRuns / inningsPitched) * 9; var rating = era < 2.5 ? 'Elite' : era < 3.5 ? 'Excellent' : era < 4.0 ? 'Good' : era < 5.0 ? 'Average' : 'Below Average'; return {era: fmt(era,2), rating: rating};

faq.a: Below 3.00 is excellent, 3.00-4.00 is good, above 5.00 is poor.
Earned runs divided by innings pitched, multiplied by 9 (a full game).

formulas.name: ERA
formulas.explanation: Earned runs per nine innings pitched.

tagline: Calculate Player Efficiency Rating for basketball
keywords: PER, basketball stats, player efficiency, NBA stats
calcFn: var raw = points + (rebounds * 1.2) + (assists * 1.5) + (steals * 2) + (blocks * 2) - turnovers; var per = (raw / minutes) * 36; var rating = per > 25 ? 'MVP Level' : per > 20 ? 'All-Star' : per > 15 ? 'Above Average' : per > 10 ? 'Average' : 'Below Average'; return {per: fmt(per,1), rating: rating};

faq.a: League average is 15. Above 20 is All-Star level, above 25 is MVP caliber.

faq.q: Is this the official PER formula?
faq.a: This is a simplified version. The official PER uses pace adjustments and league averages.

formulas.name: Simplified PER
formulas.formula: PER = (Pts + Reb×1.2 + Ast×1.5 + Stl×2 + Blk×2 - TO) / Min × 36

keywords: passer rating, QB rating, football stats, quarterback
calcFn: var a = Math.min(Math.max(((completions/attempts)-0.3)*5, 0), 2.375); var b = Math.min(Math.max(((yards/attempts)-3)*0.25, 0), 2.375); var c = Math.min(Math.max((touchdowns/attempts)*20, 0), 2.375); var d = Math.min(Math.max(2.375-((interceptions/attempts)*25), 0), 2.375); var rating = ((a+b+c+d)/6)*100; var grade = rating >= 100 ? 'Elite' : rating >= 90 ? 'Excellent' : rating >= 80 ? 'Good' : rating >= 70 ? 'Average' : 'Below Average'; return {rating: fmt(rating,1), grade: grade};

faq.q: What is a perfect passer rating?
faq.a: 158.3 is the maximum NFL passer rating.

faq.q: What is an average QB rating?
faq.a: Around 85-90 is league average in the modern NFL.

formulas.name: NFL Passer Rating
formulas.explanation: Four components: completion%, yards/att, TD%, INT%.

keywords: golf handicap, handicap index, golf score, course rating
calcFn: var diff1 = (score1 - courseRating) * 113 / slopeRating; var diff2 = (score2 - courseRating) * 113 / slopeRating; var diff3 = (score3 - courseRating) * 113 / slopeRating; var best = Math.min(diff1, diff2, diff3); var avgDiff = best; var handicap = avgDiff * 0.96; return {avgDiff: fmt(avgDiff,1), handicap: fmt(handicap,1)};

faq.a: A number representing your playing ability. Lower handicap = better golfer.

faq.q: How many rounds do I need?
faq.a: Minimum 3 rounds, officially 20 rounds for full calculation.

formulas.name: Differential
formulas.explanation: Adjusted score differential normalized by slope.

tagline: Calculate your swimming pace per 100m or 100yd
keywords: swimming pace, swim time, lap time, pool workout
calcFn: var totalSeconds = minutes * 60 + seconds; var paceSeconds = (totalSeconds / distance) * 100; var paceMin = Math.floor(paceSeconds / 60); var paceSec = Math.floor(paceSeconds % 60); var pace = paceMin + ':' + (paceSec < 10 ? '0' : '') + paceSec; var distMiles = unit === 'Meters' ? distance / 1609.34 : distance / 1760; var speed = distMiles / (totalSeconds / 3600); var laps = unit === 'Meters' ? distance / 25 : distance / 25; return {pace: pace, speed: fmt(speed,2), laps: fmt(laps,0)};

faq.a: Under 1:30 is good for recreational swimmers, under 1:00 is competitive.
Meters is used in most international competition, yards in US pools.

tagline: Calculate your watts per kilogram for cycling performance
keywords: watts per kg, cycling power, FTP, power to weight, cycling
calcFn: var weightKg = weight * 0.453592; var wpk = ftp / weightKg; var level = wpk >= 5.5 ? 'World Class' : wpk >= 4.5 ? 'Cat 1/Pro' : wpk >= 3.7 ? 'Cat 2/3' : wpk >= 3.0 ? 'Cat 4/5' : wpk >= 2.5 ? 'Recreational' : 'Beginner'; var climbSpeed = (wpk * 2.5) - 1; return {wpk: fmt(wpk,2), level: level, climbSpeed: fmt(Math.max(climbSpeed,2),1)};

faq.a: 3.0+ for fit recreational riders, 4.0+ for racing, 5.0+ for elite.
Increase FTP through training and/or reduce body weight.

formulas.name: W/kg
formulas.explanation: Functional threshold power divided by mass.

tagline: Calculate the wind chill factor temperature
keywords: wind chill, feels like, cold weather, wind chill factor
calcFn: var windChill = 35.74 + (0.6215 * temp) - (35.75 * Math.pow(wind, 0.16)) + (0.4275 * temp * Math.pow(wind, 0.16)); var danger = windChill < -40 ? 'Extreme - frostbite in <5 min' : windChill < -20 ? 'High - frostbite in 10-30 min' : windChill < 0 ? 'Moderate - dress in layers' : 'Low'; return {windChill: fmt(windChill,0), danger: danger};

faq.a: The perceived temperature accounting for wind. Wind increases heat loss from exposed skin.

faq.q: At what wind chill is it dangerous?
faq.a: Below -20°F, frostbite can occur in 10-30 minutes on exposed skin.

formulas.name: NWS Wind Chill
formulas.explanation: National Weather Service wind chill formula (T in °F, V in mph).

tagline: Calculate the feels-like temperature with humidity
keywords: heat index, feels like, humidity, heat wave, temperature
calcFn: var T = temp; var R = humidity; var hi = -42.379 + 2.04901523*T + 10.14333127*R - 0.22475541*T*R - 0.00683783*T*T - 0.05481717*R*R + 0.00122874*T*T*R + 0.00085282*T*R*R - 0.00000199*T*T*R*R; var danger = hi >= 130 ? 'Extreme Danger - heat stroke imminent' : hi >= 105 ? 'Danger - heat stroke likely' : hi >= 90 ? 'Extreme Caution' : 'Caution'; return {heatIndex: fmt(hi,0), danger: danger};

faq.a: The feels-like temperature combining heat and humidity effects on the body.
Above 105°F is dangerous, above 130°F is extremely dangerous.

formulas.name: Heat Index
formulas.explanation: Complex polynomial combining temperature and humidity.

tagline: Estimate frost line depth for your area
keywords: frost line, frost depth, foundation depth, freezing depth
calcFn: var baseDepth = (32 - avgWinterTemp) * 1.5; var snowMod = {'None': 1, 'Light (1-6 inches)': 0.75, 'Heavy (6+ inches)': 0.5}; var soilMod = {'Sandy': 1.2, 'Loam': 1, 'Clay': 0.8, 'Gravel': 1.3}; var frostDepth = baseDepth * (snowMod[snowCover]||1) * (soilMod[soilType]||1); var foundationMin = frostDepth + 6; return {frostDepth: fmt(Math.max(frostDepth,0),0), foundationMin: fmt(Math.max(foundationMin,12),0)};

faq.a: Foundations and pipes must be below the frost line to prevent heaving and freezing.

faq.q: What is the frost line in my area?
faq.a: Ranges from 0 in the South to 72+ inches in northern states. Check local building codes.

formulas.name: Frost Depth
formulas.formula: Depth = (32 - Avg Temp) × 1.5 × Snow × Soil factors
formulas.explanation: Simplified estimation based on temperature and conditions.

tagline: Calculate safe sun exposure time based on UV index
keywords: UV index, sun exposure, sunburn, SPF, UV protection
calcFn: var burnTimes = {'Type I (very fair)': 67, 'Type II (fair)': 100, 'Type III (medium)': 200, 'Type IV (olive)': 300, 'Type V (brown)': 400, 'Type VI (dark)': 500}; var base = burnTimes[skinType] || 100; var unprotected = base / uvIndex; var withSpf = spf > 0 ? unprotected * spf * 0.6 : unprotected; return {unprotected: fmt(unprotected,0), withSpf: fmt(Math.min(withSpf, 480),0)};

faq.a: 6-7 is high, 8-10 is very high, 11+ is extreme. Seek shade at 8+.
No, SPF 30 blocks 97% of UV. SPF 50 blocks 98%. Reapply every 2 hours.

formulas.name: Safe Time
formulas.formula: Minutes = Base Burn Time / UV Index × SPF × 0.6
formulas.explanation: Skin-type burn time adjusted for UV level and sunscreen.

tagline: Calculate dew point from temperature and humidity
keywords: dew point, humidity, moisture, condensation, comfort
calcFn: var tempC = (temp - 32) * 5/9; var a = 17.27; var b = 237.7; var alpha = (a * tempC)/(b + tempC) + Math.log(humidity/100); var dewC = (b * alpha)/(a - alpha); var dewPoint = dewC * 9/5 + 32; var comfort = dewPoint > 75 ? 'Extremely uncomfortable' : dewPoint > 70 ? 'Very muggy' : dewPoint > 65 ? 'Uncomfortable' : dewPoint > 60 ? 'Slightly humid' : dewPoint > 55 ? 'Comfortable' : 'Dry and pleasant'; return {dewPoint: fmt(dewPoint,0), comfort: comfort};

faq.a: The temperature at which air becomes saturated and moisture condenses.

faq.q: Why is dew point better than humidity?
faq.a: Dew point gives a more absolute measure of moisture — 65°F+ feels uncomfortable regardless of temp.

formulas.name: Magnus Formula
formulas.explanation: Approximation using temperature and relative humidity.

tagline: Forecast weather changes from barometric pressure trends
keywords: barometric pressure, weather forecast, pressure change, barometer
calcFn: var change = current - threeHoursAgo; var trend = change > 0.06 ? 'Rising rapidly' : change > 0.02 ? 'Rising slowly' : change < -0.06 ? 'Falling rapidly' : change < -0.02 ? 'Falling slowly' : 'Steady'; var forecast = change < -0.06 ? 'Storm likely approaching' : change < -0.02 ? 'Weather may deteriorate' : change > 0.06 ? 'Rapid clearing expected' : change > 0.02 ? 'Fair weather ahead' : 'Current conditions likely to continue'; return {change: (change >= 0 ? '+' : '') + fmt(change,2), trend: trend, forecast: forecast};

faq.a: 29.92 inHg (1013.25 mb) is standard sea-level pressure.

faq.q: What pressure change indicates a storm?
faq.a: A drop of 0.06+ inHg in 3 hours often signals approaching severe weather.
